title: Express Middleware (Module 2) Quiz
description: Evaluate your understanding of the orddered execution of functions and the app.use() vs route-specific middleware patterns.

questions.question: Which and/or what is the primarily role of 'Middleware' in an Express.js application?
options: A function that has access to the request (req) and response (res) objects, allowing you to run code or modify data before it reaches the final route handler.
To hide the JavaScript code from the console.

questions.question: Which method is used to register common, application-level middleware (like express.json) that runs for every request?
Which function in a middleware MUST be called to pass control to the 'next' middleware in the stack?
Is it possible to assign a specific middleware to run only for a single route (e.g., auth check for /admin)?
options: Yes, as Express supports route-level middleware definitions.
No, middleware must always be global.

questions.question: Does standard middleware like 'express.static()' allow you to serve folders of images/CSS files without writing separate route handlers?
options: Yes, as it is a built-in utility specifically for serving static assets.
